title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: vignette updates earlier tutorial developed RFmerge version 0.1-6 previous releases, relied now-superseded raster package. current workflow uses terra package demonstrates RFmerge can used create improved gridded precipitation product combining satellite-based precipitation estimates situ observations physically meaningful covariates, including elevation distances rain gauge stations. use Valparaiso Region central Chile case study. example shows generate daily merged precipitation product 0.05∘0.05^\circ spatial resolution January-August 1983. workflow requires following inputs: daily rainfall time series rain gauges, metadata describing spatial coordinates identifiers rain gauges, Climate Hazards Group InfraRed Precipitation Station data version 2.0 (CHIRPSv2), Precipitation Estimation Remotely Sensed Information using Artificial Neural Networks - Climate Data Record (PERSIANN-CDR), Shuttle Radar Topography Mission version 4 (SRTM-v4) digital elevation model (DEM). addition, distances rain gauges grid cells can used covariates. requested, distances computed internally RFmerge rain gauge station every grid cell within study area.

title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: find tutorial useful, please cite Zambrano-Bigiarini et al. (2026): Zambrano-Bigiarini, M.; Baez-Villanueva, O.M.; Giraldo-Osorio, J.D. (2026). Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0). doi:10.5281/zenodo.20061103. theoretical basis merging algorithm described following Remote Sensing Environment article: Baez-Villanueva, O. M.; Zambrano-Bigiarini, M.; Beck, H.; McNamara, .; Ribbe, L.; Nauditt, .; Birkel, C.; Verbist, K.; Giraldo-Osorio, J.D.; Thinh, N.X. (2020). RF-MEP: novel Random Forest method merging gridded precipitation products ground-based measurements, Remote Sensing Environment, 239, 111610. doi:10.1016/j.rse.2019.111606. Please also cite RFmerge R package: Zambrano-Bigiarini, M.; Baez-Villanueva, O.M., Giraldo-Osorio, J. (2026). RFmerge: Merging Satellite Datasets Ground Observations using Random Forests. R package version 0.3-3. URL: https://hzambran.github.io/RFmerge/. doi:10.32614/CRAN.package.RFmerge.

title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: tutorial developed RFmerge >= 0.3-0, first release series RFmerge based terra package. previous RFmerge versions 0.1-6 based superseded raster package. RFmerge 0.1-6 removed CRAN 2023-02-25 issues related retired spatial dependencies, rgdal, resolved time.

title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: Install latest stable version (CRAN): Alternatively, can try development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("RFmerge") if (!require(devtools)) install.packages("devtools") library(devtools) install_github("hzambran/RFmerge")

title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: Load supporting packages used analysis: Load RFmerge, provides merging function example datasets:
code: library(zoo) library(terra) library(RFmerge)

title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: example uses daily rainfall observations 34 rain gauges located Valparaiso period 1983-01-01 1983-08-31. observations available ValparaisoPPts dataset included RFmerge. applied study, equivalent dataset typically read CSV file, database export, zoo object. ValparaisoPPgis dataset contains identifiers spatial coordinates rain gauges. ValparaisoSHP object represents polygon used define outer boundary study area; user application, information commonly read shapefile, GeoPackage, another vector format supported terra. Next, load satellite-based precipitation datasets static terrain covariate. example, CHIRPSv2 (Funk et al., 2015) PERSIANN-CDR (Ashouri et al., 2015), 0.05∘0.05^\circ spatial resolution, used dynamic covariates; , dynamic means covariate varies time. SRTM-v4 DEM, also 0.05∘0.05^\circ spatial resolution, used static covariate represent influence elevation precipitation; , static means covariate vary time.
code: data(ValparaisoPPts)     data(ValparaisoPPgis) ValparaisoSHP.fname <- system.file("extdata/ValparaisoSHP.shp",package="RFmerge") ValparaisoSHP       <- terra::vect(ValparaisoSHP.fname) chirps.fname   <- system.file("extdata/CHIRPS5km.tif"       ,package="RFmerge") prsnncdr.fname <- system.file("extdata/PERSIANNcdr5km.tif"  ,package="RFmerge") dem.fname      <- system.file("extdata/ValparaisoDEM5km.tif",package="RFmerge")  CHIRPS5km        <- terra::rast(chirps.fname) PERSIANNcdr5km   <- terra::rast(prsnncdr.fname) ValparaisoDEM5km <- terra::rast(dem.fname)

title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: two multi-band GeoTIFF files provided RFmerge package store date precipitation estimate name corresponding layer. exploratory analysis, therefore assign meaningful layer names CHIRPS5km PERSIANNcdr5km: can inspect first six rows station metadata: following plot shows daily precipitation time series first rain gauge station (code: P5101005).  accumulated precipitation fields January-August 1983 provide first visual comparison CHIRPSv2 PERSIANN-CDR. study-area boundary overlaid place gridded estimates spatial context:
code: #ldates                <- hydroTSM::dip("1983-01-01", "1983-08-31") ldates                <- seq(from=as.Date("1983-01-01"), to=as.Date("1983-08-31"), by="days") names(CHIRPS5km)      <- ldates names(PERSIANNcdr5km) <- ldates head(ValparaisoPPgis) ##       Code      lon      lat            NOM_REG  NOM_PROV COD_COM     NOM_COM ## 1 P5101005 -70.8000 -32.0836 Regin de Valparaso Los Andes    5304 San Esteban ## 2 P5111002 -71.0300 -32.1567 Regin de Valparaso Los Andes    5304 San Esteban ## 3 P5101006 -70.7833 -32.1836 Regin de Valparaso Los Andes    5304 San Esteban ## 4 P5100006 -70.7839 -32.2261 Regin de Valparaso Los Andes    5304 San Esteban ## 5 P5100005 -70.7100 -32.2286 Regin de Valparaso Los Andes    5304 San Esteban ## 6 P5111001 -71.1386 -32.2528 Regin de Valparaso Los Andes    5304 San Esteban main <- paste("Daily precipitation for the station", ValparaisoPPgis$Code[1]) ylab <- "Precipitation [mm]" x.ts <- ValparaisoPPts[,1]  #hydroTSM::hydroplot(x.ts, pfreq="o", main=main, ylab= ylab) plot(x.ts, main=main, ylab= ylab, col="blue") grid() chirps.total   <- sum(CHIRPS5km, na.rm= FALSE) persiann.total <- sum(PERSIANNcdr5km, na.rm= FALSE)   plot(chirps.total, main = "CHIRPSv2 [Jan - Aug] ",       xlab = "Longitude", ylab = "Latitude",      fun=function() lines(ValparaisoSHP)) plot(persiann.total, main = "PERSIANN-CDR [Jan - Aug]",       xlab = "Longitude", ylab = "Latitude",      fun=function() lines(ValparaisoSHP))

previous_headings: Preparing input data
title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: work rain gauge locations spatial data, convert station metadata stored ValparaisoPPgis SpatVector. coordinates read lon lat columns ValparaisoPPgis data frame: can now plot DEM together rain gauge locations study-area boundary. map useful checking whether station network samples main elevation gradients across region:
code: ValparaisoPPgis.vec <- terra::vect(ValparaisoPPgis, geom=c("lon", "lat"), crs="epsg:4326") plot(ValparaisoDEM5km, main="SRTM-v4", xlab="Longitude", ylab="Latitude",       col=terrain.colors(255),      fun=function() {lines(ValparaisoSHP) ;                       points(ValparaisoPPgis.vec, pch=16, col="black")                      }     )

previous_headings: Preparing input data
title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: example produces merged precipitation product period 1983-01-01 1983-08-31, .e., 243 days. satellite precipitation products used covariates must therefore 243 layers , one day analysis period. number must match number rows ValparaisoPPts. also verify precipitation products DEM share extent, number rows columns, coordinate reference system, resolution, origin:
code: terra::nlyr(CHIRPS5km) ## [1] 243 ( terra::nlyr(CHIRPS5km) == terra::nlyr(PERSIANNcdr5km) ) ## [1] TRUE ( terra::nlyr(CHIRPS5km) == nrow(ValparaisoPPts) ) ## [1] TRUE terra::compareGeom(CHIRPS5km, PERSIANNcdr5km, ValparaisoDEM5km) ## [1] TRUE

previous_headings: Preparing input data > Verification of covariates
title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: distances used covariates RFmerge (argument ED=TRUE), natural ask whether input layers first reprojected longitude/latitude projected coordinate reference system. RFmerge relies distance calculations provided terra, whose distance documentation states: distance always expressed meter coordinate reference system longitude/latitude, map units otherwise. Map units typically meter, inspect crs(x) doubt. Results precise, sometimes much precise, using longitude/latitude rather planar coordinate reference system, distort distance. Therefore, contrast recommendation used earlier RFmerge versions based raster package, reproject main input datasets running geographical-coordinate example.

previous_headings: Running RFmerge using geographical coordinates
title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: run RFmerge, first create covariate object expected function. covariates supplied list: order names covariates list important.
code: covariates<- list(chirps=CHIRPS5km, persianncdr=PERSIANNcdr5km,                    dem=ValparaisoDEM5km)

previous_headings: Running RFmerge using geographical coordinates
title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: next decision many situ observations used train random forest model many held independent evaluation. example, 80% rain gauge stations used training remaining 20% reserved evaluation: argument seed=1000 used make random forest training station split reproducible. merged files written disk inspection reuse, set write2disk=TRUE define output directory drty.running RFmerge.
code: geoNoPar.drty.out <- file.path(tempdir(), "RFmergeResults_geographical_noparallel") system.time(rfmep.NoPar.geo <- RFmerge(x=ValparaisoPPts,                                        cov=covariates,                                       metadata=ValparaisoPPgis,                                        id="Code", lat="lat", lon="lon",                                        mask=ValparaisoSHP,                                        training=0.8,                                        seed=1000,                                       write2disk=TRUE,                                        drty.out=geoNoPar.drty.out                                       )              ) ## [ Creating the training (80%) and evaluation (20%) datasets ... ] ## Warning: [vect] guessed crs ## [ Computing the Euclidean distances to each observation of the training set ...] ##    user  system elapsed  ##  22.139   0.299  22.442

previous_headings: Running RFmerge using a projected reference system
title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: assess sensitivity workflow coordinate reference system, repeat analysis projecting inputs WGS 84 / UTM zone 19S (EPSG:32719): First, reproject gridded covariates: Second, reproject situ rain gauge stations: Third, reproject polygon used define study area: Fourth, create new data frame metadata expected RFmerge, .e., least station ID two coordinate columns. projected example, column names lon lat retained compatibility function arguments, although now contain easting northing coordinates:
code: utmz19s.p4s <- "epsg:32719" # WGS 84 / UTM zone 19S  CHIRPS5km.utm        <- terra::project(x=CHIRPS5km       , y=utmz19s.p4s) PERSIANNcdr5km.utm   <- terra::project(x=PERSIANNcdr5km  , y=utmz19s.p4s) ValparaisoDEM5km.utm <- terra::project(x=ValparaisoDEM5km, y=utmz19s.p4s) ValparaisoPPgis.vec.utm <- terra::project(x=ValparaisoPPgis.vec, y=utmz19s.p4s) ValparaisoSHP.utm <- terra::project(ValparaisoSHP, y=utmz19s.p4s) id        <- ValparaisoPPgis.vec.utm[["Code"]][,1] st.coords <- terra::crds(ValparaisoPPgis.vec.utm) lon       <- st.coords[, "x"] lat       <- st.coords[, "y"]  ValparaisoPPgis.utm <- data.frame(Code=id, lon=lon, lat=lat)

previous_headings: Running RFmerge using a projected reference system
title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: now create covariate list used RFmerge projected-coordinate example: order names covariates list important.
code: covariates.utm <- list(chirps=CHIRPS5km.utm, persianncdr=PERSIANNcdr5km.utm,                         dem=ValparaisoDEM5km.utm)

previous_headings: Running RFmerge using a projected reference system
title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: obtain results comparable geographical-coordinate run, use 80% training 20% evaluation split, controlled random seed:
code: utmNoPar.drty.out <- file.path(tempdir(), "RFmergeResults_utm_noparallel") system.time(rfmep.NoPar.utm <- RFmerge(x=ValparaisoPPts,                                        cov=covariates.utm,                                       metadata=ValparaisoPPgis.utm,                                        id="Code", lat="lat", lon="lon",                                        mask=ValparaisoSHP.utm,                                        training=0.8,                                        seed=1000,                                       write2disk=TRUE,                                        drty.out=utmNoPar.drty.out                                      )              ) ## [ Creating the training (80%) and evaluation (20%) datasets ... ] ## [ Computing the Euclidean distances to each observation of the training set ...] ##    user  system elapsed  ##  19.824   0.131  19.957

what: Running RFmerge using the parallel argument
title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: One main advantages moving RFmerge superseded raster package terra substantial improvement computational speed. larger applications, working multicore machine computing cluster, additional speed gains can obtained parallel argument RFmerge. First, define maximum number cores nodes use parallel computations: Next, detect whether operating system Windows set parallel argument accordingly: , select safe number cores comparing user-defined maximum (ncores.nmax) number cores available current environment (parallel::detectCores()). following code also avoids invalid values detectCores() returns NA one available core: can now run RFmerge parallel processing, using ncores.nmax cores:
code: ncores.nmax <- 10 # maximum amount of cores the user wants to use onWin <- ( (R.version$os=="mingw32") | (R.version$os=="mingw64") ) ifelse(onWin, parallel <- "parallelWin", parallel <- "parallel") ## [1] "parallel" ( ncores.available <- parallel::detectCores() ) ## [1] 4 if (is.na(ncores.available)) ncores.available <- 2 ( par.nnodes <- max(1, min(ncores.available - 1, ncores.nmax) ) ) ## [1] 3 geoPar.drty.out <- file.path(tempdir(), "RFmergeResults_geographical_parallel") system.time(rfmep.Par.geo <- RFmerge(x=ValparaisoPPts,                                      cov=covariates,                                     metadata=ValparaisoPPgis,                                      id="Code", lat="lat", lon="lon",                                      mask=ValparaisoSHP,                                      training=0.8,                                      seed=1000,                                     write2disk=TRUE,                                       drty.out=geoPar.drty.out,                                      parallel=parallel, par.nnodes=par.nnodes                                    )             ) ## [ Creating the training (80%) and evaluation (20%) datasets ... ] ## Warning: [vect] guessed crs ## [ Computing the Euclidean distances to each observation of the training set ...] ## [ Parallelisation finished ! ] ##    user  system elapsed  ##  40.785   1.385  18.453

title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: RFmerge runs successfully write2disk=TRUE, following outputs stored user-defined drty.directory: rain gauge stations used training evaluation; final merged product, written individual GeoTIFF files. outputs organized within drty.follows: Ground_based_data/Training/: stores time series metadata used train RF-MEP zoo file (Training_ts.txt) text file (Training_metadata.txt), respectively. Ground_based_data/Evaluation/: stores time series metadata withheld model training used independent evaluation. Evaluation_ts.txt Evaluation_metadata.txt files stored zoo CSV files, respectively. RF-MEP/: stores individual GeoTIFF files produced RF-MEP algorithm, using spatial resolution selected covariates.

previous_headings: Evaluation of RFmerge using geographical coordinates
title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: evaluate outputs obtained RFmerge geographical coordinates, use rain gauge observations withheld model training. purpose, import time series metadata evaluation stations convert station metadata spatial object:
code: ts.path       <- file.path(geoNoPar.drty.out, "Ground_based_data/Evaluation/Evaluation_ts.txt") metadata.path <- file.path(geoNoPar.drty.out, "Ground_based_data/Evaluation/Evaluation_metadata.txt")  eval.ts  <- read.zoo(ts.path, header = TRUE) eval.gis <- read.csv(metadata.path)  # Promote 'eval.gis' to a spatial object so it can be plotted. ( eval.gis.vec <- terra::vect(eval.gis, geom=c("lon", "lat")) ) ## Warning: [vect] guessed crs ## class       : SpatVector ## geometry    : points ## dimensions  : 7, 5  (geometries, attributes) ## extent      : -71.5553, -70.7247, -33.145, -32.1567  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) ## coord. ref. : +proj=longlat +datum=WGS84 +no_defs ## names       :     Code            NOM_REG  NOM_PROV COD_COM     NOM_COM ## type        :    <chr>              <chr>     <chr>   <int>       <chr> ## values      : P5111002 Regin de Valparaso Los Andes    5304 San Esteban ##               P5120003 Regin de Valparaso Los Andes    5304 San Esteban ##               P5210002 Regin de Valparaso Los Andes    5304 San Esteban ##               ...

previous_headings: Evaluation of RFmerge using geographical coordinates
what: Basic plotting of RFmerge outputs for a single day
title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: First, define colours precipitation classes used subsequent plots: following plot provides basic visualisation RF-MEP precipitation one day, 1983-01-11, study-area boundary evaluation stations overlaid:
code: minmax(rfmep.NoPar.geo[[11]])                       # range of values to be plotted ##     1983-01-11 ## min    0.34209 ## max   12.60838 breaks <- c(0, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 15, 20)         # 8 categories for the precipitation values colors <- RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(n=8, "YlGnBu")  # 8 colours plot(rfmep.NoPar.geo[[11]],      main="RF-MEP precipitation for 1983-01-11",       xlab="Longitude", ylab="Latitude",       breaks=breaks, col=colors      ) lines(ValparaisoSHP, col="black") points(eval.gis.vec, pch = 18)

previous_headings: Evaluation of RFmerge using geographical coordinates
what: Advanced plotting of RFmerge outputs for a single day
title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: informative comparison, can plot evaluation-station observations using precipitation classes colour palette RF-MEP raster: overlay study-area boundary colour evaluation stations using palette merged product. allows quick visual check closely RF-MEP field agrees observations used training.
code: stations.vals   <- as.numeric(eval.ts[11,]) stations.colors <- cut(stations.vals, breaks=breaks,                         labels=colors, include.lowest=TRUE) stations.colors <- as.character(stations.colors) # Convert factors to colour strings plot(rfmep.NoPar.geo[[11]],       main="RF-MEP precipitation for 1983-01-11",       xlab="Longitude", ylab="Latitude",       breaks=breaks, col=colors,      plg = list(title = "P, [mm]")      ) lines(ValparaisoSHP, col="black")  # Adding labels from a specific column in the SpatVector # -) labels: The name of the column in your SpatVector containing the text  #            you want to display. # -) halo  : Set to TRUE to add a buffer around the text, making it more  #            readable against complex raster backgrounds. # -) pos   : Determines the position relative to the point  #            (1=below, 2=left, 3=above, 4=right). points(eval.gis.vec, pch = 23, # Diamond with fill and border        bg = stations.colors,      # Fill colour        col = "black",             # Border (outline) colour        cex=0.9) text(eval.gis.vec, labels="Code", halo=TRUE, pos=3, cex=0.4, col="black") text(eval.gis.vec, label=paste(stations.vals, "mm"),       halo=TRUE, pos=1, cex=0.4, col="black")

previous_headings: Evaluation of RFmerge using geographical coordinates
what: Plotting the total amount of precipitation
title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: following map shows accumulated RF-MEP precipitation Valparaiso January-August 1983, study-area boundary overlaid:
code: rfmep.total <- sum(rfmep.NoPar.geo, na.rm= FALSE)  plot(rfmep.total, main = "RF-MEP [Jan - Aug]",       xlab = "Longitude", ylab = "Latitude") lines(ValparaisoSHP, lwd=1.5)

previous_headings: Evaluation of RFmerge using geographical coordinates
title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: evaluate merged product obtained RFmerge geographical coordinates, hereafter RF-MEP, together two satellite precipitation products used covariates. products compared situ rain gauge observations evaluation set. extract gridded precipitation values rain gauge locations, use extract function terra: output terra::extract includes ID column extracted points. transposition, becomes first row, must removed comparing extracted precipitation time series observed gauge records: evaluate performance RF-MEP two satellite products geographical coordinates, use Nash-Sutcliffe efficiency (NSE), implemented hydroGOF R package. NSE ranges negative infinity one, optimal value 1.
code: eval.gis.vec <- vect(eval.gis, geom = c("lon", "lat")) ## Warning: [vect] guessed crs rfmep.NoPar.geo.ts <- t(terra::extract(rfmep.NoPar.geo, eval.gis.vec)) chirps.ts         <- t(terra::extract(CHIRPS5km     , eval.gis.vec)) persiann.ts       <- t(terra::extract(PERSIANNcdr5km, eval.gis.vec)) rfmep.NoPar.geo.ts <- rfmep.NoPar.geo.ts[-1, ] chirps.ts         <- chirps.ts[-1, ] persiann.ts       <- persiann.ts[-1, ] sres       <- list(chirps.ts, persiann.ts, rfmep.NoPar.geo.ts) nsres      <- length(sres) nstations  <- ncol(eval.ts) tmp        <- rep(NA, nstations) nse.table.NoPar.geo <- data.frame(ID=eval.gis[["Code"]],                                   CHIRPS=tmp,                                   PERSIANN_CDR=tmp,                                   RF_MEP=tmp)  # Compute NSE between observed rainfall at the evaluation gauges and each # gridded product: CHIRPSv2, PERSIANN-CDR, and RF-MEP. for (i in 1:nsres) {   ldates <- time(eval.ts)   lsim   <- zoo(sres[[i]], ldates)   nse.table.NoPar.geo[, (i+1)] <- round( hydroGOF::NSE(sim= lsim, obs= eval.ts,                                                       na.rm=TRUE), 3 ) } # FOR end  # Show the final performance table for all products. nse.table.NoPar.geo ##         ID CHIRPS PERSIANN_CDR RF_MEP ## 1 P5111002  0.055        0.279  0.842 ## 2 P5120003 -0.101        0.333  0.951 ## 3 P5210002  0.166        0.378  0.934 ## 4 P5221005 -0.451        0.089  0.853 ## 5 P5421005 -1.516        0.255  0.637 ## 6 P5410008 -0.167        0.373  0.863 ## 7 P5510002  0.054        0.173  0.791

previous_headings: Evaluation of RFmerge using geographical coordinates
title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: Finally, create boxplot comparing NSE performance merged product original satellite rainfall estimates used covariates:  Higher NSE values indicate stronger agreement withheld rain gauge observations. applications sparse uneven station networks, summary interpreted together spatial diagnostics, high aggregate score can still hide local errors complex terrain coastal transition zones.
code: # Boxplot for graphical comparison. sres.cols <- c("powderblue", "palegoldenrod", "mediumseagreen") boxplot(nse.table.NoPar.geo[,2:4],          main = "Daily NSE evaluation for Jan - Aug 1983 \n using geographical coordinates",         xlab = "Precipitation products", ylab = "NSE", ylim = c(-0.5, 1),         col=sres.cols) legend("topleft", legend=c("CHIRPS", "PERSIANN-CDR", "RF-MEP"),         col=sres.cols, pch=15, cex=1.5, bty="n") grid()

previous_headings: Evaluation of RFmerge using projected coordinates
title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: evaluate outputs obtained RFmerge projected (UTM) coordinates, use rain gauge observations withheld model training. import evaluation time series metadata, convert station metadata projected spatial object:
code: ts.path       <- file.path(utmNoPar.drty.out, "Ground_based_data/Evaluation/Evaluation_ts.txt") metadata.path <- file.path(utmNoPar.drty.out, "Ground_based_data/Evaluation/Evaluation_metadata.txt")  eval.ts      <- read.zoo(ts.path, header = TRUE) eval.gis.utm <- read.csv(metadata.path)  # Promote 'eval.gis.utm' to a spatial object so it can be plotted. ( eval.gis.vec.utm <- terra::vect(eval.gis.utm, geom=c("lon", "lat"), crs=utmz19s.p4s ) ) ## class       : SpatVector ## geometry    : points ## dimensions  : 7, 1  (geometries, attributes) ## extent      : 261653.7, 338417.6, 6329731, 6440390  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) ## coord. ref. : WGS 84 / UTM zone 19S (EPSG:32719) ## names       :     Code ## type        :    <chr> ## values      : P5111002 ##               P5120003 ##               P5210002 ##               ...

what: Advanced plotting of RFmerge outputs for a single day
title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: informative comparison, plot evaluation-station observations using precipitation classes colour palette RF-MEP raster: overlay study-area boundary colour evaluation stations using palette merged product. allows quick visual check closely projected-coordinate RF-MEP field agrees observations used training.
code: stations.vals   <- as.numeric(eval.ts[11,]) stations.colors <- cut(stations.vals, breaks=breaks,                         labels=colors, include.lowest=TRUE) stations.colors <- as.character(stations.colors) # Convert factors to colour strings plot(rfmep.NoPar.utm[[11]],       main="RF-MEP precipitation for 1983-01-11",       xlab="Easting", ylab="Northing",       breaks=breaks, col=colors,      plg = list(title = "P, [mm]")      ) lines(ValparaisoSHP.utm, col="black")  # Adding labels from a specific column in the SpatVector # -) labels: The name of the column in your SpatVector containing the text  #            you want to display. # -) halo  : Set to TRUE to add a buffer around the text, making it more  #            readable against complex raster backgrounds. # -) pos   : Determines the position relative to the point  #            (1=below, 2=left, 3=above, 4=right). points(eval.gis.vec.utm, pch = 23, # Diamond with fill and border        bg = stations.colors,      # Fill colour        col = "black",             # Border (outline) colour        cex=0.9) text(eval.gis.vec.utm, labels="Code", halo=TRUE, pos=3, cex=0.4, col="black") text(eval.gis.vec.utm, label=paste(stations.vals, "mm"),       halo=TRUE, pos=1, cex=0.4, col="black")

code: rfmep.total.utm <- sum(rfmep.NoPar.utm, na.rm= FALSE)  plot(rfmep.total.utm, main = "RF-MEP [Jan - Aug]",       xlab = "Easting", ylab = "Northing") lines(ValparaisoSHP.utm, lwd=1.5)

previous_headings: Evaluation of RFmerge using projected coordinates
title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: evaluate merged product obtained RFmerge projected coordinates, hereafter RF-MEP, together two satellite precipitation products used covariates. products compared situ rain gauge observations evaluation set. extract gridded precipitation values rain gauge locations, use extract function terra: output terra::extract includes ID column extracted points. transposition, becomes first row, must removed comparing extracted precipitation time series observed gauge records: evaluate performance RF-MEP two satellite products projected coordinates, use Nash-Sutcliffe efficiency (NSE), implemented hydroGOF R package. NSE ranges negative infinity one, optimal value 1.
code: rfmep.NoPar.utm.ts <- t(terra::extract(rfmep.NoPar.utm   , eval.gis.vec.utm)) chirps.ts.utm      <- t(terra::extract(CHIRPS5km.utm     , eval.gis.vec.utm)) persiann.ts.utm    <- t(terra::extract(PERSIANNcdr5km.utm, eval.gis.vec.utm)) rfmep.NoPar.utm.ts <- rfmep.NoPar.utm.ts[-1, ] chirps.ts.utm     <- chirps.ts.utm[-1, ] persiann.ts.utm   <- persiann.ts.utm[-1, ] sres       <- list(chirps.ts.utm, persiann.ts.utm, rfmep.NoPar.utm.ts) nsres      <- length(sres) nstations  <- ncol(eval.ts) tmp        <- rep(NA, nstations) nse.table.NoPar.utm <- data.frame(ID=eval.gis.utm[["Code"]],                                   CHIRPS=tmp,                                   PERSIANN_CDR=tmp,                                   RF_MEP=tmp)  # Compute NSE between observed rainfall at the evaluation gauges and each # gridded product: CHIRPSv2, PERSIANN-CDR, and RF-MEP. for (i in 1:nsres) {   ldates <- time(eval.ts)   lsim   <- zoo(sres[[i]], ldates)   nse.table.NoPar.utm[, (i+1)] <- round( hydroGOF::NSE(sim= lsim, obs= eval.ts,                                                       na.rm=TRUE), 3 ) } # FOR end  # Show the final performance table for all products. nse.table.NoPar.utm ##         ID CHIRPS PERSIANN_CDR RF_MEP ## 1 P5111002  0.009        0.283  0.832 ## 2 P5120003 -0.052        0.338  0.950 ## 3 P5210002  0.178        0.384  0.932 ## 4 P5221005 -0.549        0.091  0.860 ## 5 P5421005 -0.386        0.248  0.615 ## 6 P5410008  0.129        0.370  0.838 ## 7 P5510002  0.041        0.182  0.788

previous_headings: Evaluation of RFmerge using projected coordinates
title: Merging satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge (>=0.3-0)
text: Finally, create boxplot comparing NSE performance merged product original satellite rainfall estimates used covariates:  second evaluation useful checking whether reprojection materially changes merged product extracted values station locations. practice, geographical-coordinate workflow usually preferable distance calculations terra, projected-coordinate comparison can helpful project requires consistency existing GIS layers regional modelling workflows.
code: # Boxplot for graphical comparison. sres.cols <- c("powderblue", "palegoldenrod", "mediumseagreen") boxplot(nse.table.NoPar.utm[,2:4],          main = "Daily NSE evaluation for Jan - Aug 1983 \n using projected coordinates",         xlab = "Precipitation products", ylab = "NSE", ylim = c(-0.5, 1),         col=sres.cols) legend("topleft", legend=c("CHIRPS", "PERSIANN-CDR", "RF-MEP"),         col=sres.cols, pch=15, cex=1.5, bty="n") grid()

what: Merging of Satellite Datasets with Ground Observations using Random Forests
title: Merging of Satellite Datasets with Ground Observations using Random Forests
text: RFmerge R package precipitation merging, environmental data fusion, bias correction gridded datasets, Random Forest-based spatio-temporal prediction using rain gauges, satellite products, reanalysis data, topography, Euclidean-distance covariates.

title: Merging of Satellite Datasets with Ground Observations using Random Forests
text: RFmerge R package merging gridded environmental products ground-based observations generate spatially consistent, analysis-ready datasets. implements Random Forest-based MErging Procedure (RF-MEP), machine-learning framework introduced Báez-Villanueva et al. (2020) improve spatio-temporal representation precipitation combining rain-gauge measurements, multiple gridded products, topography-related predictors within unified Random Forest model. methodological motivation straightforward: single source fully characterises environmental variability. Ground stations provide high-quality point measurements sparse spatial coverage, whereas satellite, reanalysis, gridded products provide spatial continuity often exhibit systematic bias, missed events, scale-dependent errors. RFmerge exploits complementary information contained sources produce merged fields better represent spatial patterns, temporal dynamics, event occurrence, particular value complex terrain data-scarce regions. RF-MEP, separate Random Forest model fitted time step using station observations response variable gridded covariates predictors. covariates may include satellite reanalysis products, digital elevation models, Euclidean-distance layers account proximity observation sites. design allows RFmerge capture non-linear relations precipitation environmental controls preserving flexibility across daily, monthly, seasonal, annual applications. approach originally developed evaluated precipitation merging, RFmerge outperformed individual input products, alternative merging methods, , cases, benchmark merged dataset MSWEPv2.2 across multiple temporal scales. framework can also adapted environmental variables quality-controlled ground observations suitable gridded covariates available. hydrology, climatology, remote sensing, water-resources research, RFmerge provides reproducible extensible workflow bias-aware environmental data fusion. especially useful researchers seeking improved gridded datasets hydrological modelling, climate diagnostics, hazard analysis, environmental monitoring.

previous_headings: Spatial metadata
title: Merging of Satellite Datasets with Ground Observations using Random Forests
text: Reprojecting input datsets geographic coordinates WGS 84 / UTM zone 19S (EPSG:32719): Creating new data.frame expected metadata, .e., least, ID, lat, lon:
code: utmz19s.p4s <- "epsg:32719" # WGS 84 / UTM zone 19S  CHIRPS5km.utm        <- terra::project(x=CHIRPS5km       , y=utmz19s.p4s) PERSIANNcdr5km.utm   <- terra::project(x=PERSIANNcdr5km  , y=utmz19s.p4s) ValparaisoDEM5km.utm <- terra::project(x=ValparaisoDEM5km, y=utmz19s.p4s)  stations.utm         <- terra::project(x=stations, y=utmz19s.p4s) ValparaisoSHP.utm    <- terra::project(ValparaisoSHP, y=utmz19s.p4s) id        <- stations.utm[["Code"]][,1]  st.coords <- terra::crds(stations.utm) lon       <- st.coords[, "x"] lat       <- st.coords[, "y"]  ValparaisoPPgis.utm <- data.frame(ID=id, lon=lon, lat=lat)

what: Merging of Satellite Datasets with Ground Observations using Random Forests — RFmerge-package
title: Merging of Satellite Datasets with Ground Observations using Random Forests — RFmerge-package
text: S3 implementation Random Forest MErging Procedure (RF-MEP), combines two satellite-based datasets (e.g., precipitation products, topography) ground observations produce new dataset improved spatio-temporal distribution target field. particular, package developed merge different Satellite-based Rainfall Estimates (SREs) measurements rain gauges, order obtain new precipitation dataset time series rain gauges used correct different types errors present SREs. However, package might used merge hydrological/environmental satellite fields point observations. details, see Baez-Villanueva et al. (2020) <doi:10.1016/j.rse.2019.111606>. Bugs / comments / questions / collaboration kind welcomed.

title: Merging of Satellite Datasets with Ground Observations using Random Forests — RFmerge-package

title: Merging of Satellite Datasets with Ground Observations using Random Forests — RFmerge-package
text: Baez-Villanueva, O. M.; Zambrano-Bigiarini, M.; Beck, H.; McNamara, .; Ribbe, L.; Nauditt, .; Birkel, C.; Verbist, K.; Giraldo-Osorio, J.D.; Thinh, N.X. (2020). RF-MEP: novel Random Forest method merging gridded precipitation products ground-based measurements, Remote Sensing Environment, 239, 111610. doi:10.1016/j.rse.2019.111606 . <https://doi.org/10.1016/j.rse.2019.111606>. Hengl, T., Nussbaum, M., Wright, M. N., Heuvelink, G. B., & Gräler, B. (2018). Random forest generic framework predictive modeling spatial spatio-temporal variables. PeerJ, 6, e5518. doi:10.7717/peerj.5518 .

title: Merging of Satellite Datasets with Ground Observations using Random Forests — RFmerge-package
code: library(terra) #> terra 1.9.25  data(ValparaisoPPts)     data(ValparaisoPPgis)   ValparaisoSHP.fname <- system.file("extdata/ValparaisoSHP.shp",package="RFmerge") ValparaisoSHP       <- terra::vect(ValparaisoSHP.fname)    chirps.fname   <- system.file("extdata/CHIRPS5km.tif",package="RFmerge") prsnncdr.fname <- system.file("extdata/PERSIANNcdr5km.tif",package="RFmerge") dem.fname      <- system.file("extdata/ValparaisoDEM5km.tif",package="RFmerge")  CHIRPS5km        <- rast(chirps.fname) PERSIANNcdr5km   <- rast(prsnncdr.fname) ValparaisoDEM5km <- rast(dem.fname)  covariates <- list(chirps=CHIRPS5km, persianncdr=PERSIANNcdr5km,                     dem=ValparaisoDEM5km)  # \donttest{  # The following code assumes that the region is small enough for neglecting # the impact of computing Euclidean distances in geographical coordinates. # If this is not the case, please read the vignette 'Tutorial for merging  # satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge'   # without using parallelisation rfmep <- RFmerge(x=ValparaisoPPts, metadata=ValparaisoPPgis, cov=covariates,                  id="Code", lat="lat", lon="lon", mask=ValparaisoSHP, training=1) #> [ Creating the training (100%) and evaluation (0%) datasets ... ] #> Warning: [vect] guessed crs #> [ Computing the Euclidean distances to each observation of the training set ...]  # Detecting if your OS is Windows or GNU/Linux,  # and setting the 'parallel' argument accordingly: onWin <- ( (R.version$os=="mingw32") | (R.version$os=="mingw64") ) ifelse(onWin, parallel <- "parallelWin", parallel <- "parallel") #> [1] "parallel"  #Using parallelisation, with a maximum number of nodes/cores to be used equal to 2: par.nnodes <- min(parallel::detectCores()-1, 2) rfmep <- RFmerge(x=ValparaisoPPts, metadata=ValparaisoPPgis, cov=covariates,                  id="Code", lat="lat", lon="lon", mask=ValparaisoSHP,                   training=0.8, parallel=parallel, par.nnodes=par.nnodes) #> [ Creating the training (80%) and evaluation (20%) datasets ... ] #> Warning: [vect] guessed crs #> [ Computing the Euclidean distances to each observation of the training set ...] #> [ Parallelisation finished ! ] # }

title: Merging of satellite datasets with ground observations using Random Forests (RF) — RFmerge
code: RFmerge(x, ...)  # Default S3 method RFmerge(x, metadata, cov, mask, training,          id="id", lat = "lat", lon = "lon",          ED = TRUE, rasterizedED=FALSE,          seed = NULL, ntree = 2000, na.action = stats::na.omit,         parallel=c("none", "parallel", "parallelWin"),         par.nnodes=parallel::detectCores()-1,          par.pkgs= c("terra", "randomForest", "zoo"), write2disk=FALSE,         drty.out, use.pb=TRUE, verbose=TRUE,...)  # S3 method for class 'zoo' RFmerge(x, metadata, cov, mask, training,          id="id", lat = "lat", lon = "lon",          ED = TRUE, rasterizedED=FALSE,          seed = NULL, ntree = 2000, na.action = stats::na.omit,         parallel=c("none", "parallel", "parallelWin"),         par.nnodes=parallel::detectCores()-1,          par.pkgs= c("terra", "randomForest", "zoo"), write2disk=FALSE,         drty.out, use.pb=TRUE, verbose=TRUE, ...)

title: Merging of satellite datasets with ground observations using Random Forests (RF) — RFmerge
text: x data.frame ground-based values used dependent variable train RF model.  Every column must represent one ground-based station codes stations must provided colnames. class(data) must zoo. metadata data.frame metadata ground-based stations.  least, MUST following 3 columns: -) id: column stores unique identifier (ID) ground-based observation. Default value "id".  -) lat: column stores latitude ground observation. Default value "lat".  -) lon: column stores longitude ground observation. Default value "lon". cov List covariates used independent variables Random Forest model. individual covariates must SpatRaster objects, either vary time (e.g., individual gridded precipitation datasets) vary time (e.g., digital elevation model).  time-varying covariates cov MUST number layers (bands), internally checked using , nlyr function. Covariates change time (e.g., DEM) internally transformed SpatRaster objects number layers time-varying elements cov. mask OPTIONAL. provided, final merged product mask values cov outside mask.  Spatial object (vectorial) spatial borders study area (e.g., catchment, administrative borders). class(mask) must SpatVect object "POLYGON" "MULTIPOLYGON" geometry. training Numeric indicating percentage stations used training set.  valid range zero one. training = 1, stations used training purposes. id Character, name column metadata identification code (ID) station stored. lat Character, name column metadata latitude stations stored. lon Character, name column metadata longitude stations stored. ED logical, Euclidean distances computed used covariates random forest model?. default value TRUE. rasterizedED logical, Euclidean distances stations grid cells computed actual point coordinate (rasterizedED=FALSE) rasterized station cell center (rasterizedED=TRUE). rasterizedED=FALSE, self-distance station grid cell usually zero. default, rasterizedED=FALSE. seed Numeric, indicating  single value, interpreted integer, null. parallel character, indicates parallelise ‘RFmerge’ (precise, evaluation objective function fn parallelised). Valid values : -)none: parallelisation made (default value) -)parallel: parallel computations network clusters machines multiple cores CPUs. ‘FORK’ cluster created makeForkCluster function. -)parallelWin: parallel computations network clusters machines multiple cores CPUs (parallel implementation works Windows machines). ‘PSOCK’ cluster created makeCluster function. par.nnodes OPTIONAL. Used parallel!='none' numeric, indicates number cores/CPUs used local multi-core machine, number nodes used network cluster. default par.nnodes set amount cores detected function detectCores() (parallel package) par.pkgs OPTIONAL. Used parallel='parallelWin' list package names (characters) need loaded node allowing objective function fn evaluated. default c("terra", "randomForest", "zoo"). ntree Numeric indicating maximum number trees grow Random Forest algorithm. default value set 2000. set small number, ensure every input row gets predicted least times. value low, prediction may biased. na.action function specify action taken NAs found.  (NOTE: given, argument must named.) write2disk logical, indicates output merged SpatRaster layers training evaluation datasets (two files , one time series metadata) written disk. default write2disk=FALSE drty.Character full path directory final merged product exported well training evaluation datasets. used write2disk=TRUE use.pb logical, indicates progress bar used show progress random forest computations (might reduce bit performance computations, useful track everything working well). default use.pb=TRUE verbose logical, indicates progress messages printed. default verbose=TRUE ... arguments passed low level function randomForest.default.

title: Merging of satellite datasets with ground observations using Random Forests (RF) — RFmerge

title: Merging of satellite datasets with ground observations using Random Forests (RF) — RFmerge
text: Baez-Villanueva, O. M.; Zambrano-Bigiarini, M.; Beck, H.; McNamara, .; Ribbe, L.; Nauditt, .; Birkel, C.; Verbist, K.; Giraldo-Osorio, J.D.; Thinh, N.X. (2020). RF-MEP: novel Random Forest method merging gridded precipitation products ground-based measurements. Remote Sensing Environment, 239, 111610. doi:10.1016/j.rse.2019.111606 . Hengl, T.; Nussbaum, M.; Wright, M. N.; Heuvelink, G. B.; Gräler, B. (2018). Random forest generic framework predictive modeling spatial spatio-temporal variables. PeerJ, 6, e5518. doi:10.7717/peerj.5518 .

title: Merging of satellite datasets with ground observations using Random Forests (RF) — RFmerge
text: Oscar M. Baez-Villanueva, oscar.baezvillanueva@ugent. Mauricio Zambrano-Bigiarini, mzb.devel@gmail    Juan D. Giraldo-Osorio, j.giraldoo@javeriana.edu.co

title: Merging of satellite datasets with ground observations using Random Forests (RF) — RFmerge
code: library(terra)  data(ValparaisoPPts)     data(ValparaisoPPgis)      ValparaisoSHP.fname <- system.file("extdata/ValparaisoSHP.shp",package="RFmerge") ValparaisoSHP       <- terra::vect(ValparaisoSHP.fname)  chirps.fname   <- system.file("extdata/CHIRPS5km.tif",package="RFmerge") prsnncdr.fname <- system.file("extdata/PERSIANNcdr5km.tif",package="RFmerge") dem.fname      <- system.file("extdata/ValparaisoDEM5km.tif",package="RFmerge")  CHIRPS5km        <- rast(chirps.fname) PERSIANNcdr5km   <- rast(prsnncdr.fname) ValparaisoDEM5km <- rast(dem.fname)  covariates <- list(chirps=CHIRPS5km, persianncdr=PERSIANNcdr5km,                     dem=ValparaisoDEM5km)  # \donttest{  # The following code assumes that the region is small enough for neglecting # the impact of computing Euclidean distances in geographical coordinates. # If this is not the case, please read the vignette 'Tutorial for merging  # satellite-based precipitation datasets with ground observations using RFmerge'    # without using parallelisation rfmep <- RFmerge(x=ValparaisoPPts, metadata=ValparaisoPPgis, cov=covariates,                  id="Code", lat="lat", lon="lon", mask=ValparaisoSHP, training=1) #> [ Creating the training (100%) and evaluation (0%) datasets ... ] #> Warning: [vect] guessed crs #> [ Computing the Euclidean distances to each observation of the training set ...]   # Detecting if your OS is Windows or GNU/Linux,  # and setting the 'parallel' argument accordingly: onWin <- ( (R.version$os=="mingw32") | (R.version$os=="mingw64") ) ifelse(onWin, parallel <- "parallelWin", parallel <- "parallel") #> [1] "parallel"  #Using parallelisation, with a maximum number of nodes/cores to be used equal to 2: par.nnodes <- min(parallel::detectCores()-1, 2) rfmep <- RFmerge(x=ValparaisoPPts, metadata=ValparaisoPPgis, cov=covariates,                  id="Code", lat="lat", lon="lon", mask=ValparaisoSHP,                   training=0.8, parallel=parallel, par.nnodes=par.nnodes) #> [ Creating the training (80%) and evaluation (20%) datasets ... ] #> Warning: [vect] guessed crs #> [ Computing the Euclidean distances to each observation of the training set ...] #> [ Parallelisation finished ! ] # }

title: Spatial location of rain gauges in the Valparaiso region (Chile) — ValparaisoPPgis
text: Projection: EPSG:4326

title: Spatial location of rain gauges in the Valparaiso region (Chile) — ValparaisoPPgis
text: data.frame seven fields:  *) 'ID             : identifier gauging station.  *) 'STATION_NAME'  : name gauging station.  *) 'lon'           : easting coordinate gauging station, EPSG:4326.  *) 'lat'           : northing coordinate gauging station, EPSG:4326.  *) 'ELEVATION'     : elevation gauging station, [m .s.l.].  *) 'BASIN_ID'      : identifier subbasin gauging station s located.  *) 'BASIN_NAME'    : name subbasin gauging station s located.

title: Spatial location of rain gauges in the Valparaiso region (Chile) — ValparaisoPPgis
text: Downloaded ('Red de Control Meteorologico') web site Confederacion Hidrografica del Ebro (CHE) http://www.chebro.es/ (original link http://oph.chebro.es/ContenidoCartoClimatologia.htm, last accessed [March 2008]), name 7 selected fields translated English language. data intended used research purposes , distributed hope useful, WITHOUT WARRANTY.

text: CR2 dataset unifies individual datasets provided Dirección General de Aguas (DGA) Dirección Meteorológica de Chile (DMC), Chilean water meteorological agencies, respectively.  data intended used research purposes , distributed hope useful, WITHOUT WARRANTY.

title: Changes in version 0.3-0 01-May-2026
code: o RFmerge now has a graphical logo.       o RFmerge now has a webpage (https://hzambran.github.io/RFmerge/), created by pkgdown.       o CITATION file: Updated style The DOI was changed form the one given by Zotero to the new DOI that will be given by CRAN (10.32614/CRAN.package.RFmerge).     o The 'parallel' option is working now (based ont he 'terra' package), thank to the asssistance of Codex (ChatGPT).     o Several small enhancements and quality control changes.

title: Changes in version 0.2-0 26-Jul-2023 (not public)
code: o The package was completely checked and modified when deemed appropiate in order **to make it work with the 'terra' pacakge instead of the superseeded 'raster' package**. This was motivated by a tehnical course OScr Baez-Villanueva an I gave to SISSA in Buenos Aires (Argentina). However, the parallel option did not work due to problems with the use of SpatRast and SpatVec objects in the terra package. For this reason, this version remained only on Github, without being submitted to CRAN.

title: Changes in version 0.1-10 21-May-2020
text: CRAN release: 2020-05-22

title: Changes in version 0.1-10 21-May-2020
code: o Updated version after CRAN reported an unnecessary 'RFmerge-internal.Rd' file, which was removed for this release.

title: Changes in version 0.1-9 12-May-2020
code: o Updated version after CRAN reported an error in the vignette ("Invalid argument: 'cov' and 'mask' have different CRS !!"). this error was previously undetected because the package was tested with R-devel in combination with GEOS 3.5.1, GDAL 2.2.2, PROJ 4.9.2, while CRAN uses GEOS 3.8.1, GDAL 3.0.4, PROJ 7.0.0.

title: Changes in version 0.1-8 28-Apr-2020 (not public)
code: o Updated version after CRAN reported a problem with the vignette when tested against R-devel.

title: Changes in version 0.1-7 27-Apr-2020 (not public)
code: o 'rfmerge': sf::st_crs(x])[["epsg"]] was changed to 'sf::st_crs(x)$proj4string' for identifying the projection of a vectorial or raster object. This was due to a report from Edzer Pebema mentioning that the old call used a legacy 'crc' structure, which was changed in the new verison of the 'sf' package.

title: Changes in version 0.1-6 07-Jan-2020
text: CRAN release: 2020-01-09

title: Changes in version 0.1-6 07-Jan-2020
code: o 'rmarkdown' was added to 'Suggests' after failing a new CRAN test.     o the minimal vignette was modified to automatically set the 'parallel' argumento to 'paralell' or 'parallelWin' depending whether the OS is based on GNU/Linux or Windows, respectively.

title: Changes in version 0.1-5 04-Jan-2020
code: o 'RFmerge-package.Rd': now the example correctly uses 2 cores as maximum when package is tested with the "--run-donttest" option

title: Changes in version 0.1-4 04-Jan-2020
code: o DOI was added to the CITATION file and all the references within the package (the article was available online in January 2nd).     o A maximum of two cores is used in all examples (fixed, with respect to the previous version).     o Improved vignettes (minor details).

title: Changes in version 0.1-3 20-Dec-2019
code: o 'RFmerge.R': new argument 'write2disk' to allow the user to choose whether the output merged layers should be written to disk or not. Default value is 'FALSE'.      o 'RFmerge.R': 'installed.packages()' was replaced by 'find.package', for increasing efficiency to find out if a named package is installed or not.     o Examples are now run within a 'donttest{}' piece of code instead of a '\dontrun{}' one.     o A maximum of two cores is used in all examples, even if the number of cores available is much larger than that (required by CRAN)     o RFmerge.Rd: '\value' tag was added, explaining the returning object of the function.     o Hengl et al. (2018) was added as a reference.

title: Changes in version 0.1-2 18-Dec-2019
code: o The package was built using the '--compact-vignettes="gs+qpdf"'  to avoid the following WARNIG from CRAN: "'gs+qpdf' made some significant size reductions".

title: Changes in version 0.1-1 18-Dec-2019
code: o Full PDF vignette was replaced by a  minimal RMarkdown version to avoid dependencies from rgdal, hydroTSM, and hydroGOF.

title: Changes in version 0.1-0 15-Dec-2019
code: o First release     o It depends on R >= 3.5.0 because serialized objects (.rds data files) in serialize/load version 3 cannot be read in older versions     o RMarkdown vignette was replaced by a sttic PDF  one to avoid dependencies from rgdal, hydroTSM, and hydroGOF.
